title: dactylorhiza traunsteineri var. estonica
intro: Dactylorhiza traunsteineri var. estonica, also known as the Estonian marsh orchid, is a subspecies of the Dactylorhiza traunsteineri orchid, a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza traunsteineri var. estonica are said to include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, and it is also used in herbal medicine in the form of extracts and decoctions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
